Final answer: \boxed{153}

The initial difference is 250. After 2 hours, the difference is 20, so the decay factor is 20/250^(1/2). Thus H(t)=250*(20/250)^(t/2). The first quarter hour drop is H(0.25)-H(0), which is 250*(20/250)^(0.25)-250. The next quarter hour drop is H(0.5)-H(0.25), which is 250*(20/250)^(0.5)-250*(20/250)^(0.25). The inverse function is H^(-1)(y)=log(y/250)/log(20/250)*2. The time for 5 degrees is log(5/250)/log(20/250)*2.

Final answers, in order: \boxed{250*(20/250)**(t/2), 250*(20/250)**(0.25)-250, 250*(20/250)**(0.5)-250*(20/250)**(0.25), log(y/250)/log(20/250)*2, log(5/250)/log(20/250)*2}
